review: Spotify is 100% my favorite music app. It’s amazing because not only does it have any music you can think of, but you can watch any podcast and sometimes even YouTube channels you want to! This app also has the AI DJ feature that takes all the music you are listening to at the moment and plays it. It also shows you new songs that you may like. And last but certainly not least, Spotify lets you follow artists on the app so that if any new information about these artists (new songs, concerts near you, new music videos, etc.) comes out, the app sends you a notification! This app is truly terrific and even better with the Premium Spotify subscription. Although it costs money, it is one of the best things I have ever invested in. This allows you to have unlimited skips, no advertisements, etc. One of the best decisions I have ever made is downloading Spotify and subscribing to Spotify Premium and you should do the same!

I shouldn’t have to pay $14 just to play my own playlist normally that’s a basic feature that should be available to everyone. I understand paying for premium to remove ads or get extra perks, but restricting something as simple as choosing and playing your own songs is frustrating. On top of that, it often just plays one song and then ignores the rest of the playlist I picked, which makes the experience even worse. It has a lot of potential, but these limitations really hold it back.

review: Allowing AI-generated music onto your platform is an affront to every human musician that has ever uploaded to Spotify and thus led to its incredible success. In my opinion, every moment Spotify delays in identifying and culling AI-generated music from its platform is a blatant pronouncement that money is more important than music and hopefully takes it a step or three closer to boycott or mass legal action. A sad thing to wish upon the greatest thing to happen to music in the 21st century. But there it is. Spotify, please come back…

I’m unable to view podcasts or most of my song library because it takes an incredibly long time for the screen to buffer, despite me having selected lower media quality levels and having my library downloaded. The app struggles to be functional now, which is way more important to me than having built in AI to make subpar playlists. If having new tools means sacrificing the functionality of the very basics of the app (playing music) then there’s no use for the app at all. Will be looking to switch streaming platforms after 10+ years of only using Spotify.

review: I love Spotify as much as the next guy and what I’ve been really excited to get for Spotify, is lossless audio. However, if you download way too many songs in lossless quality, it essentially eats your entire phone’s storage. So like any rational person, I downgraded the quality of my downloads to very high, but thought since I’m 99% of the time listening to music through WiFi or cellular data, that I could turn that to Lossless and would get that quality. Unfortunately, Spotify doesn’t work that way. So any song that I like enough to download to my phone, even if I’m connected to the internet and should have Lossless audio for it, is instead placed in Very High quality which is kind of frustrating. Just because I would like to listen to my favorite songs in Lossless, but also would like to have access to them in a slightly lesser quality in case I’m not connected to the internet without it eating my phones entire storage.
title: Download quality is prioritized over best possible quality at any given moment

review:
Spotify has genuinely become one of the most important parts of my everyday life. Music isn’t just background noise to me — it’s therapy, healing, motivation, comfort, memories, and sometimes the only thing that understands emotions words can’t explain. Through every heartbreak, celebration, long drive, workout, work shift, sleepless night, and life-changing moment, Spotify has been there with the perfect song at the perfect time.

What I love most about Spotify is how personal it feels. The playlists, recommendations, daylists, and discover features somehow always know exactly what my soul needs to hear. One moment I’m dancing and feeling unstoppable, the next I’m crying to lyrics that feel like they were written directly from my heart. Music has the power to save people, and Spotify gives millions of people access to that feeling every single day.

The app itself is smooth, organized, and easy to use, but beyond the technology, it creates connection. Spotify helped me discover artists from all over the world, songs tied to memories I’ll never forget, and music that got me through some of the hardest periods of my life. There are songs I physically cannot listen to without remembering certain people, places, or moments — and that’s beautiful. That’s powerful.

I truly believe music is one of the keys to life. It brings people together regardless of language, culture, pain, or background. Spotify made music accessible in a way that transformed daily living for so many of us. Whether I’m driving by the ocean at night, getting ready for work at 4 AM, healing from loss, or celebrating life with friends, Spotify is always part of the experience.

Thank you for creating something that does more than stream songs. You created a platform that holds emotions, memories, healing, energy, love, and human connection. I honestly cannot imagine life without music, and I cannot imagine music without Spotify.
title: Love the vibes

Besides, the cost is just getting ridiculous at this point. 20 bucks a month for two people is INSANE. Removing feature after feature that made the app great and users enjoyed gets pretty grating. After this payment cycle I’m switching over to YouTube music, where I can pay the same price for 5 people and spilt the payment between us. Not to mention, there’s wayyyy more music there because they actually pay the artists decent royalties!

review: Love Spotify for the music. Was paying for the Spotify duo for my daughter and I, no problem, decided I wanted to try audiobooks, even though I’m a premium member you have to pay to use that service, so I said why not?! Added the audiobooks for an extra $12 a month, but guess what? You only get 15 hours of audiobooks, but don’t worry, you can pay more to get more time…. With the recent price change it went up to a total of $33 a month. That’s more than a tv/video streaming service. Honestly, made me want to cancel my plan all together but I hate Apple Music and pandora so I just got rid of audiobooks. All around annoyed at paying premium prices for mediocre benefits.

This app used to be great, but they keep changing trying to include functionality that is not needed. If by any chance you click on the song, it does not maximize, the song takes only half the screen, then you have to click on the song AGAIN so this time it maximizes.
